servinfo.title: Main Bearings, Crankshaft and Cylinder Block Removal and Installation

ptxt.intxrefpoff: Main Bearing, Crankshaft and Cylinder Block Components

attention2.ptxt: Parts must go back in the position where they were. Keep them in the following order:

s1.ptxt: Remove engine assembly from vehicle.
Remove flywheel.
Remove pistons and connecting rods.
Remove engine rear crankshaft oil seal housing (1).
Remove main bearing cap bolts in numerical order gradually as indicated in figure.
Remove crankshaft from cylinder block.
Remove sensor plate from crankshaft, if necessary.

item.ptxt: Apply engine oil to the following parts:
Do not apply engine oil to the following spots:
Mating surfaces between main bearing and cylinder block
Mating surfaces between main bearing and main bearing cap
Mating surfaces between connecting rod bearing and connecting rod
Mating surfaces between connecting rod bearing and connecting rod cap
Install the following parts in the position where they were:

s1.ptxt: Install sensor plate (1) to crankshaft (3) and tighten bolts to specified torque, if removed.

item.ptxt: Align spring pin hole (4) on sensor plate with spring pin (2) on crankshaft.
Inside edge (5) of sensor plate is bent. Install sensor plate properly.

s1.ptxt: Install main bearings to cylinder block and main bearing cap.

item.ptxt: Install main bearing halves with oil groove to cylinder block.
Install other main bearing halves to main bearing cap.

s2.ptxt: Fit the tab (1) on each bearing into recess (2) in cylinder block and main bearing cap.
Press bearing end (3) until it fully seat in cylinder block and main bearing cap.
Apply engine oil to sliding surface (4) of main bearing halves.

attention2.ptxt: Do not apply engine oil to mating surface (5) between bearing half and cylinder block or main bearing cap.

s1.ptxt: Install thrust bearings (1) to cylinder block between No.2 and No.3 cylinders.

attention3.ptxt: Be sure to face oil groove (2) side to crank web.

s1.ptxt: Confirm that dowel pins (3) are installed to intake side of each journal.
Install crankshaft to cylinder block.
Install bearing cap to cylinder block, making sure to point arrow mark (on each cap) to crankshaft pulley side. Fit them sequentially in ascending order, 1, 2, 3, 4 and R, starting from pulley side.
After applying engine oil to main bearing cap bolts (“1” through “10”) tighten them as follows.

attention3.ptxt: If main bearing cap bolt is reused, check main bearing cap bolt for deformation referring to “Main bearing cap bolt” under Main Bearings Inspection .

s2.ptxt: Tighten bolts to 20 N·m (2.0 kgf-m, 15.0 lbf-ft) according to numerical order as shown in figure using a 12-point socket wrench.
In the same manner as Step a), tighten them to 40 N·m (4.0 kgf-m, 29.5 lbf-ft).
In the same manner as Step a), retighten them to 60°.

attention2.ptxt: After tightening cap bolts, check to be sure that crankshaft rotates smoothly when turning it by 12 N·m (1.2 kgf-m, 9.0 lbf-ft) torque or below.

s1.ptxt: Clean sealing surface between oil seal housing (2) and cylinder block.
Apply engine oil to new rear oil seal (1) and install it to engine rear crankshaft oil seal housing (2) using special tool.

spec.title: Engine rear crankshaft oil seal installing position (dimension)

s1.ptxt: Install dowel pin (2) to rear oil seal housing (1) as shown in figure.
Apply sealant to mating surface of rear oil seal housing (1).

spec.title: Sealant amount for rear oil seal housing

s1.ptxt: Apply engine oil to oil seal lip, then install engine rear crankshaft oil seal housing (1) using special tool.
Tighten new engine rear crankshaft oil seal housing bolts (2) to specified torque.

attention3.ptxt: Confirm that engine rear crankshaft oil seal lip is not turned over after engine rear crankshaft oil seal housing is installed.

torqueitem.t-part: Engine rear crankshaft oil seal housing bolt

s1.ptxt: Install flywheel.
Install pistons and connecting rods.
Install cylinder head.
Install camshafts and tappets.
Install timing chain.
Install timing chain cover.
Install cylinder head cover.
Install oil pan.
Install engine assembly to vehicle.
